title: Standards/curriculum mapping + coverage measurement for the subject
context: After batch-001 publishes the first set of decks, it is important to measure whether the deck set provides meaningful curriculum coverage relative to a recognized standard. Without a standards mapping, it is impossible to know what fraction of the target curriculum the decks cover, or where the gaps are. A reproducible coverage measurement method enables tracking progress over time.
objective: Map the chosen subject's deck set to a recognized open curriculum standard and measure coverage (cards covered / total in-scope learning objectives), using a reproducible method.
acceptanceCriteria: Identifies a publicly available, recognized curriculum standard or syllabus for the chosen subject (name, version, URL, access date).
Maps each published deck's cards to the standard's learning objectives or topic list.
Measures coverage as (cards covering an objective / total in-scope objectives) with a clear denominator and methodology.
Identifies coverage gaps and suggests which topics to prioritize in future batch tasks.
Documents the methodology reproducibly so coverage can be re-measured after each batch run.
Published as a versioned document, CC-BY-4.0.
output: A versioned curriculum-mapping and coverage-measurement report (docs/curriculum-coverage.md) for the chosen subject, with methodology, coverage delta, and gap analysis, CC-BY-4.0.
